content.text:
Now I have enough data to trace the top 3 positions across all years. Let me compile:

**Color key (from labels):**
- Lettuce = dark navy blue
- Sour cream = medium blue
- Pico de gallo = lighter blue
- Avocado = peach/salmon
- Tomato = dark navy (similar to Lettuce)

**Top 3 by year:**
| Year | #1 | #2 | #3 |
|------|----|----|-----|
| 2015 | Lettuce (dark navy) | Sour cream (med blue) | Tomato (dark navy) |
| 2016 | Lettuce (dark navy) | Sour cream (med blue) | Avocado (peach) |
| 2017 | Pico de gallo (light blue) | Lettuce (dark navy) | Sour cream (med blue) |
| 2018 | Pico de gallo (light blue) | Sour cream (med blue) | Avocado (peach) |
| 2019 | Pico de gallo (light blue) | Sour cream (med blue) | Lettuce/Tomato (dark navy) |

**Top-3 appearance counts:**
- Sour cream: 5 years
- Lettuce: 4–5 years
- Pico de gallo: 3 years
- Avocado: 2 years
- Tomato: 1–2 years

<think>
Sour cream appeared in the top 3 all 5 years (always at rank 2 or 3). Lettuce appeared at least 4 years (rank 1 in 2015-2016, rank 2 in 2017, possibly rank 3 in 2019, dropped to ~4 in 2018). Pico de gallo rose dramatically to rank 1 from 2017-2019 (3 years). Avocado appeared in 2016 and 2018 (2 years). Tomato appeared only in 2015 and possibly 2019.
</think>

<answer>
Lettuce and Sour cream appeared in the top 3 most often — both appeared in the top 3 for virtually every year shown (2015–2019). Sour cream held a top-3 spot all 5 years (consistently at rank 2 or 3), and Lettuce held a top-3 spot for at least 4 of the 5 years (rank 1 from 2015–2016, rank 2 in 2017, and returning to rank 3 in 2019). Pico de gallo came next with 3 years in the top 3 (2017–2019, rising to rank 1), followed by Avocado (2 years: 2016 and 2018), and Tomato (only 2015 after "taking a dive").
</answer>
